habitat: Evergreen forests; open woodland; mixed forest; and hilly/mountainous areas at 300–1800 m
story: The Reeves Pheasant (Syrmaticus reevesii) is an extraordinary bird that belongs to the Phasianidae family. It is most commonly observed in Evergreen forests; open woodland; mixed forest; and hilly/mountainous areas at 300–1800 m, where its behaviors contribute to the ecological dynamics of the area. Listed as Vulnerable (VU) by the IUCN, this species' future depends on continued conservation action. The story of the Reeves Pheasant is one of adaptation and resilience, offering valuable lessons for conservationists. This poster aims to bring the Reeves Pheasant into focus, highlighting its beauty and the importance of its habitat.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
